query: I want to connect Claude Code to my EKS clusters. Should I use the self-hosted EKS MCP Server or the AWS-hosted one?
Walk me through installing the awslabs eks-mcp-server locally with uvx and an AWS profile.
What IAM policy do I need to attach to run the self-hosted EKS MCP Server in IAM mode?
I added the eks-mcp config to my mcp.json but when I restart Claude Code no EKS tools show up. What's wrong?
Where does the mcp.json file live for Amazon Q CLI vs Claude Code vs Cursor? I want the EKS MCP server available in all of them.
My EKS MCP Server keeps failing with an AccessDenied on eks-mcp:InvokeMcp. How do I fix this?
Should I put the EKS MCP server config in project-scope .mcp.json or user-scope ~/.claude/mcp.json?
How do I configure the self-hosted EKS MCP Server in kubeconfig mode so I don't need AWS IAM credentials?
List all the node groups and Karpenter NodePools in my prod-use1 cluster and tell me what instance families they use.
What version is my EKS cluster on and which add-ons are installed?
Do a full inventory of my EKS cluster — compute, networking, add-ons, observability — I need context before an upgrade.
Check whether my clusters are using IMDSv2 and report any nodes still on v1.
We're building a new multi-tenant EKS platform. Should we use Karpenter, managed node groups, or Auto Mode for the tenant workloads?
How do I write my own MCP server in Python using the official SDK? I want to expose an internal API as tools.
Assess my cluster's upgrade readiness and produce a scored report with blockers — can we move to 1.32?
Give me an operational review of my EKS cluster across all 10 standard areas with a maturity score.
Wire up a self-service portal so developers can provision AWS resources via ACK without raising infra tickets.
